===Pokémon===
[[File:Cilan Party.png|thumb|250px|left|Cilan's current [[party]]]]
During his time running the Striaton Gym with his brothers, Cilan usually specialized in {{type|Grass}} Pokémon. However, he diversified during his travels with Ash, noticeably catching Pokémon that are strong against opponents that have the advantage over Grass-type, such as his {{TP|Cilan|Crustle}} and {{TP|Cilan|Stunfisk}}, who are both strong against {{t|Fire}} and {{type|Flying}} Pokémon.

Although he doesn't do a lot of training with his Pokémon, they seems to be capable battlers, as they have helped Cilan in the many tournaments he participated in.

This listing is of Cilan's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}:

====Released====
{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Cilan
|pkmn=Basculin
|type1=Water
|img=Basculin Blue-Striped anime.png
|epnum=BW037
|epname=A Fishing Connoisseur in a Fishy Competition!
|vaen=Tom Wayland
|vajp=Unshō Ishizuka
|desc=In ''[[BW037|A Fishing Connoisseur in a Fishy Competition!]]'', Cilan, during a fake fishing competition hosted by {{TRT}}, hooked a {{p|Basculin|Blue-Striped Basculin}} and battled it with his {{TP|Cilan|Pansage}}. Basculin fought well, but it was quickly defeated by Pansage's {{m|Solar Beam}}. Cilan promptly caught it in a Fishing Poké Ball and handed it to [[Jessie|one of the contest officials]] for judging. Later on, Team Rocket took all of the competitors' caught Pokémon, and attempted to escape with them. However, a female {{p|Frillish}} managed to release all the caught Pokémon, including Basculin.

Basculin's known moves are {{m|Water Gun}}, {{m|Bite}}, and {{m|Aqua Tail}}.}}
